extra.message: User-supplied 'filter' argument is interpolated directly into a raw SQL string passed to SQLAlchemy's text() function via Python string formatting: `text("title = '%s' or content = '%s'" % (filter, filter))`. An attacker can craft the filter value to escape the string literal and inject arbitrary SQL.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: The ImportPaste mutation constructs a shell command by concatenating user-controlled values (host, port, path, scheme) into an f-string URL and passing it to helpers.run_cmd() which calls os.popen(). Even though strip_dangerous_characters() is called in easy mode it returns the input unchanged, and in hard mode it only strips ';' and '&', leaving injection vectors such as backtick substitution, $() or newlines. An attacker controls all four URL components and can achieve OS command injection.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: resolve_system_debug() passes the user-supplied 'arg' argument directly to os.popen() via helpers.run_cmd('ps {}'.format(arg)) with no sanitisation whatsoever. An attacker can append shell metacharacters (e.g. `; id`) to arg to execute arbitrary OS commands.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: resolve_system_diagnostics() calls helpers.run_cmd(cmd) where 'cmd' is a user-supplied GraphQL argument. Although security.allowed_cmds() is checked, in easy mode (helpers.is_level_easy()) it unconditionally returns True, meaning all commands are allowed. The command string is passed to os.popen() with no shell-metacharacter filtering, enabling OS command injection.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: The ImportPaste mutation constructs a URL from four fully user-controlled GraphQL arguments (scheme, host, port, path) and makes an outbound HTTP request via `curl --insecure` through os.popen(). No whitelist or network-level restriction on the target is enforced. An attacker can direct the server to make requests to internal network resources (e.g. http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/) constituting a Server-Side Request Forgery.

save_file() constructs a file path by concatenating WEB_UPLOADDIR with a user-supplied filename string (WEB_UPLOADDIR + filename) without any path normalisation, canonicalisation, or validation. An attacker can supply a filename such as '../../etc/cron.d/backdoor' to write arbitrary files anywhere on the filesystem that the process user can access.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: get_identity() decodes a JWT token with `verify_signature: False` and `verify_exp: False`. This means any crafted or expired JWT is accepted as valid. An attacker can forge a JWT with any identity (e.g. 'admin') without knowing the secret key, bypassing authentication entirely.

cwe: CWE-347: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature

extra.message: resolve_password() in UserObject returns the plaintext password when info.context.json.get('identity') == 'admin'. Because get_identity() does not verify JWT signatures (see helpers.py:21), any requester can forge an 'admin' identity token and retrieve all users' plaintext passwords via the GraphQL API.

DeletePaste mutation accepts an integer id and deletes the corresponding paste with no authentication or ownership check. Any unauthenticated user can delete any paste by ID, constituting both a missing authentication and an IDOR vulnerability.

EditPaste mutation accepts a paste id and new title/content with no authentication or ownership verification. Any unauthenticated user can overwrite the title and content of any paste by its numeric id, constituting an Insecure Direct Object Reference.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: resolve_delete_all_pastes() deletes every paste in the database with no authentication check. Any anonymous requester can irreversibly wipe all paste data.

resolve_users() returns all user records (including usernames and—depending on the caller's identity—passwords) without any authentication requirement. An unauthenticated attacker can enumerate all user accounts.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The Login mutation queries the database for a user by username and plaintext password (`filter_by(username=username, password=password)`). Passwords are stored and compared in plaintext — no hashing (bcrypt, PBKDF2, etc.) is used anywhere in the codebase. This means a database breach immediately exposes all credentials.

Both SECRET_KEY and JWT_SECRET_KEY are hardcoded to the string 'dvga'. A static, publicly known secret key allows attackers to forge Flask session cookies and JWT tokens, completely bypassing authentication. Note also that app.secret_key is set to os.urandom(24) on line 10 and then immediately overwritten by the hardcoded value on line 14.

The admin account is seeded with the weak, hardcoded password 'changeme' (the only option in the weak_passwords list). The operator account is seeded with the hardcoded password 'password123' (line 64). Both passwords are trivially guessable and stored in plaintext.

format_custom_error() exposes full Python stack traces, the absolute server-side file path of view_override.py, and the output of traceback.format_exc() in the GraphQL error response under extensions.exception. This discloses internal application structure, file system paths, and potentially sensitive variable values to any API consumer.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: resolve_pastes() accepts a user-supplied 'limit' argument with a default of 1000 and no maximum cap. An attacker can request an arbitrarily large number of rows (e.g. limit=2147483647) causing excessive database load and memory consumption, constituting a Denial of Service.

simulate_load() is called synchronously from resolve_system_update() and deliberately sleeps for 20–50 seconds (0.1s * 200–500 iterations). Any authenticated (or unauthenticated in easy mode) call to the systemUpdate query will block a gevent worker for the duration, enabling a trivial Denial of Service against the server.

IGQLProtectionMiddleware grants access to the GraphiQL IDE when the client-controlled cookie 'env' equals 'graphiql:enable'. Cookies are trivially forgeable by the client. An attacker simply sets document.cookie='env=graphiql:enable' to bypass the GraphiQL access control entirely.

The before_request hook reads the X-DVGA-MODE HTTP header and uses it to switch the server's security mode between 'easy' (all protections disabled) and 'hard'. Any unauthenticated client can send this header on every request to ensure all security controls (depth limit, cost limit, introspection block, denylist) are disabled, bypassing all hardening measures.

resolve_audits() returns the full audit log — including all GraphQL queries ever executed — to any unauthenticated requester. This can expose credential-bearing queries, internal operation names, and patterns of use that aid further attacks.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
